title: eremophila fraseri
intro: Eremophila fraseri, also known as Fraser's emu bush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae, native to Western Australia. It has been used in traditional Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Eremophila fraseri include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations. Eremophila fraseri is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in topical creams and ointments to harness its medicinal benefits.
